topic: CIC, CVC, Lokpal, CBI, NIA and NITI Aayog
subtopic: Information, vigilance, anti-corruption, investigation, national security and policy coordination bodies

content.issueStory: Bodies commonly grouped as watchdogs differ radically in legal source, jurisdiction, coercive power, appointment, independence and accountability. CIC, CVC, Lokpal, CBI, NIA and NITI Aayog are not constitutional bodies. They arise from different statutes or executive resolution and perform information adjudication, vigilance, anti-corruption inquiry, police investigation, national-security investigation or policy coordination.
content.coreConcept: CIC, CVC, Lokpal, CBI, NIA and NITI Aayog are not constitutional bodies. They arise from different statutes or executive resolution and perform information adjudication, vigilance, anti-corruption inquiry, police investigation, national-security investigation or policy coordination.
dimensions: CIC operates under right-to-information statute and adjudicates information appeals and complaints within law.
CVC is a statutory vigilance body with supervisory and advisory functions under its Act.
Lokpal is a statutory anti-corruption ombudsman with defined jurisdiction and process.
CBI derives police powers through the DSPE framework and faces federal-consent and court-direction questions.
NIA is a statutory investigation agency for scheduled offences with special jurisdiction rules.
NITI Aayog is an executive policy institution for cooperative planning and advice, not a constitutional finance body.
content.constitutionalLink: Right to Information Act; Central Vigilance Commission Act; Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act; DSPE Act; NIA Act; NITI Aayog executive resolution.
content.dataReport: Uploaded source basis: STATIC_09_Polity.pdf and PYQ_06_Polity_Governance.pdf. Refresh amendments, statutory rules, office status and later judgments from official sources: India Code and official CIC, CVC, Lokpal, CBI, NIA and NITI Aayog portals.
content.caseStudy: CBI investigation within a State can engage statutory consent and federalism, while constitutional courts retain powers to direct investigation under doctrine.
argumentsAgainst: Executive appointment and control affect independence.
Vacancies and weak follow-up reduce impact.
content.pyqLink: UPSC patterns test Article-institution matches, exceptions, procedure and comparative statements within CIC, CVC, Lokpal, CBI, NIA and NITI Aayog. Resolve each statement through source, scope, procedure and consequence.

answerFramework.intro: Introduce CIC, CVC, Lokpal, CBI, NIA and NITI Aayog through the controlling constitutional principle and legal source.
body: Map the authority: Right to Information Act; Central Vigilance Commission Act; Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act; DSPE Act; NIA Act; NITI Aayog executive resolution.

Explain the first-order design: CIC operates under right-to-information statute and adjudicates information appeals and complaints within law. CVC is a statutory vigilance body with supervisory and advisory functions under its Act. Lokpal is a statutory anti-corruption ombudsman with defined jurisdiction and process.

Analyse safeguards and tensions: CBI derives police powers through the DSPE framework and faces federal-consent and court-direction questions. NIA is a statutory investigation agency for scheduled offences with special jurisdiction rules. NITI Aayog is an executive policy institution for cooperative planning and advice, not a constitutional finance body.

Use CBI investigation within a State can engage statutory consent and federalism, while constitutional courts retain powers to direct investigation under doctrine. and close with constitutional accountability plus dated legal verification.
answerFramework.conclusion: A strong Polity answer joins constitutional text, institutional purpose, judicial doctrine and a workable accountability safeguard.

mainsExamples: NITI Aayog coordinating policy without allocating Finance Commission transfers.
prelimsFacts: These bodies are not all constitutional.
NITI Aayog was created by executive resolution.

revisionBox.prompt: What is the controlling rule in CIC, CVC, Lokpal, CBI, NIA and NITI Aayog?
revisionBox.answer: CIC, CVC, Lokpal, CBI, NIA and NITI Aayog are not constitutional bodies. They arise from different statutes or executive resolution and perform information adjudication, vigilance, anti-corruption inquiry, police investigation, national-security investigation or policy coordination.

revisionBox.prompt: What is the primary constitutional or legal authority?
revisionBox.answer: Right to Information Act; Central Vigilance Commission Act; Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act; DSPE Act; NIA Act; NITI Aayog executive resolution.

revisionBox.prompt: What is the fastest elimination trap?
revisionBox.answer: Do not call NITI Aayog constitutional.

revisionBox.prompt: Where should later legal changes be verified?
revisionBox.answer: India Code and official CIC, CVC, Lokpal, CBI, NIA and NITI Aayog portals

story: Bodies commonly grouped as watchdogs differ radically in legal source, jurisdiction, coercive power, appointment, independence and accountability.
core_concept: CIC, CVC, Lokpal, CBI, NIA and NITI Aayog are not constitutional bodies. They arise from different statutes or executive resolution and perform information adjudication, vigilance, anti-corruption inquiry, police investigation, national-security investigation or policy coordination.

mindmap_json.center: CIC, CVC, Lokpal, CBI, NIA and NITI Aayog

branches.label: Text
children: CIC operates under right-to-information statute and adjudicates information appeals and complaints within law.
CVC is a statutory vigilance body with supervisory and advisory functions under its Act.

branches.label: Institution
children: Lokpal is a statutory anti-corruption ombudsman with defined jurisdiction and process.
CBI derives police powers through the DSPE framework and faces federal-consent and court-direction questions.

branches.label: Safeguard
children: NIA is a statutory investigation agency for scheduled offences with special jurisdiction rules.
NITI Aayog is an executive policy institution for cooperative planning and advice, not a constitutional finance body.

branches.label: Traps
children: Do not call NITI Aayog constitutional.
Do not give CIC criminal investigative power.
Do not treat CVC, Lokpal and CBI as identical bodies.

key_facts: These bodies are not all constitutional.
NITI Aayog was created by executive resolution.
common_traps: Do not call NITI Aayog constitutional.
Do not give CIC criminal investigative power.
Do not treat CVC, Lokpal and CBI as identical bodies.

pyq_refs.answer: Check text, scope, exception and competent authority.

pyq_refs.pattern_note: Mains rewards constitutional balance over one-sided description.
